title: Shri Devi Kavacham
intro: Read Shri Devi Kavacham with Hindi meaning. Chant this sacred hymn to seek the protection and blessings of Maa Durga.
description:
॥ Shri Durga Kavacham ॥

॥ Markandeya Uvacha ॥
1. Yad guhyam paramam loke sarvarakshakaram nrinaam.
Yanna kasyachidakhyatam tanme bruhi pitamaha.

॥ Brahmovacha ॥
2. Asti guhyatamam vipra sarvabhutopakarakam.
Devyastu kavacham punyam tacchrinushva mahamune.
3-5. Prathamam Shailaputriti dvitiyam Brahmacharini.
Tritiyam Chandraghanteti Kushmandeti chaturthakam.
Panchamam Skandamateti shashtham Katyayaniti cha.
Saptamam Kalaratrishcha Mahagauriti chashtamam.
Navamam Siddhidatri cha Navadurgah prakirtitah.
Uktanyetani namani Brahmanaiva mahatmana.
6-7. Agnina dahyamanastu shatrumadhye gato rane.
Vishame durgame chaiva bhayartah sharanam gatah.
Na tesham jayate kinchit ashubham ranasankate.
Napadam tasya pashyami shokaduhkhabhayam na hi.
8. Yaistu bhaktya smrita nunam tesham siddhih prajayate.
Pretasamstha tu Chamunda Varahi Mahishasana.
9-10. Aindri gajasamarudha Vaishnavi Garudasana.
Maheshvari vrisharudha Kaumari Shikhivahana.
Brahmi hamsasamarudha sarvabharanabhushita.
Nanabharanashobhadhya nanaratnopashobhitah.
11-12. Drishyante rathamarudha devyah krodhasamakulah.
Shankham chakram gadam shaktim halam cha musalayudham.
Khetakam tomaram chaiva parashum pashameva cha.
Kuntayudham trishulam cha Sharngamayudhamuttamam.
13. Daityanam dehanashaya bhaktanamabhayaya cha.
Dharayantyayudhanittham devanam cha hitaya vai.
14. Mahabale mahotsahe mahabhayavinashini.
Trahi mam devi dushprekshye shatrunam bhayavardhini.
15-16. Prachyam rakshatu mamaindri Agneyyam Agnidevata.
Dakshine-vatu Varahi Nairrityam khadgadharini.
Prachyam Varuni rakset Vayavyam mrigavahini.
Udichyam raksha Kauberi Ishanyam shuladharini.
17. Urdhvam Brahmani me rakset adhastad Vaishnavi tatha.
Evam dasha disho rakset Chamunda shavavahana.
18. Jaya me chagratah statu Vijaya statu prishthatah.
Ajita vamaparshve tu dakshine chaparajita.
19-20. Shikham me dyotini rakset Uma murdhni vyavasthita.
Maladhari lalate cha bhruvau rakshed-yashasvini.
Trinetra cha bhruvormadhye Yamaghanta cha nasike.
Shankhini chakshushormadhye shrotrayordvaravasini.
21-22. Kapolau Kalika raksetkarnamule tu Shankari.
Nasikayam sugandha cha uttaroshthe cha Charchika.
Adhare chamritakala jihvayam cha Sarasvati.
Dantan rakshatu Kaumari kantham madhyetu Chandika.
23-24. Ghantikam Chitraghanta cha Mahamaya cha taluke.
Kamakshi chibukam rakset vacham me sarvamangala.
Grivayam Bhadrakali cha prishthavamshe Dhanurdhari.
Nilagriva bahihkanthe nalikam Nalakubari.
25-26. Khadgadharinyu bhau skandho bahu me Vajradharini.
Hastayordandini rakset Ambika changulistha.
Nakhan-Shuleshvari rakset-kukshau rakset-Naleshvari.
Stanau raksen-Mahalakshmih manah shokavinashini.
27-28. Hridayam Lalita Devi udaram Shuladharini.
Nabhau cha Kamini rakset guhyam Guhyeshvari tatha.
Katyam Bhagavati rakset-januni Vindhyavasini.
Bhutanatha cha medram me uru Mahishavahini.
29-30. Janghe Mahabala prokta sarvakama pradayini.
Gulphayornarasimhi cha padau chamita-taijasi.
Padangulih Shrirme rakset-padadhastalavasini.
Nakhan-danshtrakarali cha keshanshchaivordhvakeshini.
31-32. Romakupeshu Kauberi tvacham Vagishvari tatha.
Raktamajjavasamamsani asthimedamsi Parvati.
Antrani Kalaratrishcha pittam cha Mukuteshvari.
Padmavati padmakoshe kaphe Chudamanistatha.
33-34. Jvalamukhi nakhajvala abhedya sarvasandhishu.
Shukram Brahmani me rakset chhaya Chatreshvari tatha.
Ahankaram mano buddhim rakshame Dharmacharini.
Pranapanau tatha vyanam samanodanameva cha.
35-36. Yashah kirtincha Lakshmincha sada rakshatu Vaishnavi.
Gotram Indrani me rakset pashunme raksha Chandike.
Putran raksen-Mahalakshmih bharyam rakshatu Bhairavi.
Margam Kshemakari rakset Vijaya sarvatah sthita.
37-38. Rakshahinam tu yatsthanam varjitam kavachena tu.
Tatsarvam raksha me devi Jayanti papanashini.
Padamekam na gachchettu yadicchecchubhamatmanah.
Kavachenavrito nityam yatra yatradhigacchati.
39-41. Tatra tatrarthalabhashcha vijayah sarvakamikah.
Yam yam kamayate kamam tam tam prapnoti nishchitam.
Paramaishvaryamatulam prapsyate bhutale puman.
Nirbhayah jayate martyah sangramesvaparajitah.
Trailokye tu bhavetpujyah kavachenavrito puman.
Idam tu Devyah kavacham devanamapi durlabham.
42-43. Yah pathetprayato nityam trisandhyam shraddhayanvitah.
Daivi kala bhavettasya trailokye-paparajitah.
Jived-varshashatam sagram-apamrityuvivarjitah.
Nashyanti vyadhayah sarve lutavisphotakadayah.
44-47. Sthavaram jangamam vapi kritrimam chapi yadvisham.
Abhicharani sarvani mantrayantrani bhutale.
Bhucharah khecharashchaiva jalajashopadeshikah.
Sahajah kulajah malah Shakini Dakini tatha.
Antarikshachara ghora dakinyashcha mahabalah.
Grahabhutapishachashcha yakshagandharvarakshasah.
Brahmarakshasavetalah Kushmanda Bhairavadayah.
Nashyanti darshanattasya kavache hridi samsthite.
48-50. Manonnatirbhaved-rajnah tejovriddhikaram param.
Yashasa vardhate sopi kirtimanditabhutale.
Japetsaptashatim Chandim kritva tu kavacham pura.
Yavad-bhumandalam dhatte sashailavanakananam.
Tavattishthati medinyam santatih putrapautriki.
Dehante paramam sthanam yatsurairapi durlabham.
Prapnoti purusho nityam Mahamaya prasadatah.

॥ Iti Shri Durga Kavacham Sampurnam ॥

faq.q1: What is the Shri Durga Kavacham?
faq.a1: The Shri Durga Kavacham is a sacred and powerful protective hymn found in the Durga Saptashati (part of the Markandeya Purana). It is called a "Kavach" (armor) because it is believed to create a divine protective shield around the devotee.
faq.a2: It is recited to seek the protection of Goddess Durga against all kinds of dangers, evil spirits, enemies, and negativity. It invokes divine blessings for fearlessness, courage, and mental strength.
faq.q3: Is it mandatory to recite the Kavach during the Durga Saptashati?
faq.a3: Yes, in the traditional ritual of the Durga Saptashati (Chandi Path), the Durga Kavacham is recited first to create a protective space before beginning the main prayers.
faq.q4: What are the benefits of regular recitation?
faq.a4: Regular recitation is believed to protect the devotee from illnesses, negative energy, and fear. It bestows peace of mind, confidence, and victory over obstacles in life.
faq.q5: Is there a specific protocol for reciting it?
faq.a5: It should be recited with a clean body, a focused mind, and deep devotion. Ideally, one should sit in front of an idol or picture of Goddess Durga and recite it with sincerity.
